dependencies: Lithium concentrate from Windfield joint venture (Greenbushes mine)
Lithium concentrate from MARBL joint venture (Wodgina mine)
Lithium brine from Salar de Atacama, Chile
Lithium brine from Silver Peak, Nevada
Bromine from Dead Sea (Jordan Bromine Company Limited joint venture)
risks: Concentration risk in lithium supply from joint ventures
Dependence on spodumene from Australia (Greenbushes and Wodgina)
Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions
Climate change impacts on resource extraction

regulatory_risks: REACH regulations in European Union requiring comprehensive chemical reporting and authorization
Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) requirements in United States
Environmental regulations on discharge of pollutants and hazardous waste management
Occupational Safety and Health Act (OSHA) compliance
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) violations and anti-bribery laws
Export control and economic embargo regulations
Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act import restrictions
Climate change regulations including carbon pricing and cap-and-trade legislation
Pillar Two Global Anti-Base Erosion Rules (15% global minimum tax)
Chemical Facility Anti-Terrorism Standards (CFATS) program requirements
Mining and environmental permits for resource extraction operations
Water usage restrictions in high water-risk areas
Brominated flame retardant regulations and potential bans
Data privacy regulations including EU General Data Protection Regulation
Indigenous land rights and community agreements
Tax law changes including Inflation Reduction Act impacts

sanction_risks: US-China trade tensions and tariffs on electric vehicles and lithium-ion batteries
Export control restrictions on technology transfer to prohibited countries
Economic sanctions limiting business in certain jurisdictions
Potential tariffs on Chinese imports announced by US administration
Trade restrictions affecting raw material and product flows
Russia-Ukraine conflict impacts on global supply chains and energy costs
Middle East geopolitical instability affecting Jordan operations
Nationalization or expropriation risks in foreign jurisdictions
Currency exchange controls and capital repatriation restrictions
